name: Physiotherapy
description: Refer yourself for assessment and treatment of musculoskeletal problems — back pain, joint pain, sports injuries, and more.
howToRefer: Call Fife Community Physiotherapy or use the online self-referral form.

name: Mental health (Talking therapies / IAPT)
description: Access counselling, cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT), and other talking therapies for anxiety, depression, and stress.
howToRefer: Refer yourself online via NHS Fife Psychological Therapies.

name: Smoking cessation
description: Get free NHS support to stop smoking, including nicotine replacement therapy and one-to-one advice.
howToRefer: Call the Quit Your Way Fife service or speak to your pharmacist.

name: Weight management
description: Access NHS weight management support, including dietary advice and structured programmes.
howToRefer: Speak to your pharmacist or contact Counterweight / NHS Fife dietetics.

name: Sexual health
description: Confidential sexual health services including STI testing, contraception advice, and treatment.
howToRefer: Contact Fife Sexual Health service directly — no GP referral needed.

name: Podiatry (foot care)
description: For conditions affecting your feet and lower limbs. Self-refer if you have diabetes or a long-term condition.

name: Optometry (eye care)
description: Get a free NHS eye test at any registered optician. They can also refer you for further treatment if needed.
howToRefer: Book directly with any NHS-registered optician.

name: Speech and language therapy
description: Support for adults with communication difficulties, swallowing problems, or voice disorders.
howToRefer: Contact NHS Fife Speech and Language Therapy.

name: Community nursing (district nursing)
description: Nursing care at home for patients who cannot easily attend the surgery — wound care, medication support, and more.
howToRefer: Call our practice and we'll arrange a referral to the district nursing team.

name: Occupational therapy
description: Support to help you manage everyday activities if you have a long-term health condition or disability.
howToRefer: Contact Fife Council Social Work for community OT, or NHS Fife for clinical OT.
